message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Aim</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The objective of this analysis was to assess the association of excess COVID-19 mortality with regulation enforcement and vaccination rate in selected countries.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    This analysis included 50 countries pertinent to the WHO European Region, in addition to USA and Canada. Excess mortality and vaccination data were retrieved from
                    <jats:italic>“Our World In Data”</jats:italic>
                    database, while regulation implementation was measured from a well-respected, standardized measure. Outpatient visits were also included in the analysis. Multiple linear regression was used to assess the independent association between excess mortality and each covariate.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Excess mortality increased by 4.1/100 000 for every percent decrease in vaccination rate and with 6/100 000 for every decreased unit in the regulatory implementation score a country achieved in the Rule of Law Index.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Degree of regulation enforcement, likely including public health measure enforcement, may be an important factor in controlling COVID-19’s deleterious health impacts.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: COVID-19 excess death rate in Eastern European countries associated with weaker regulation implementation and lower vaccination coverage

reference.article-title: The association between early countryLlevel COVIDL19 testing capacity and later COVIDL19 mortality outcomes

reference.unstructured: Eurostat. Excess mortality starting to fall back in 2021, after a large death toll. https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/products-eurostat-news/-/ddn-20210416-2 (accessed 6 February 2022).
Statens Serum Institut, Denmark. EUROMOMO. https://euromomo.eu/.

reference.article-title: Challenges encountered in comparing international policy responses to COVID-19 and their effects
